content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 15, 1989


Howard L. McCalla
2650 Calle De Compadres
Alpine, CA 92001

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-565

Dear Mr. McCalla:

You have requested advice under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

The Commission's Legal Division provides written advice to
persons with questions about their duties under the Act. We do
not provide advice to third parties about another person's duties
or obligations unless the third party has been authorized to
request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in
question. (Section 83114, Regulation 18329; copy enclosed.)
Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the question you have
raised. However, if you believe a violation of the Act has
occurred, you may contact our Enforcement Division at
(916) 322-6441. A copy of a complaint for is enclosed for your
convenience.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

Enclosure


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

HOWARD L. McCALLA
2650 CALLE DE COMPADRES
ALPINE, CALIF. 92001

(619) 445-5738


September 26, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: John Larsen, Chairman
428 "J" Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

SUBJECT: Request for ruling on application of the term
"assuming office" [Gov. Code Section 87302(b)].

Dear Mr. Larsen,

On July 18, 1989 the San Diego County Board of Supervisors
filled an existing vacancy on the Alpine Planning Group by
appointing an Alpine resident named Ulf Brynjestad. [See
attached Board minute order #66.] Financial disclosure state-
ments are required under Section 87302(b) "within 30 days of
assuming office...". The purpose of this letter is to request
an FPPC ruling on when this appointee is deemed to have
"assumed office". Was it the date of his appointment or was it
the date he attended his first meeting as a member.

The appointee attended his first meeting on July 27, 1989,
the regular meeting for that month. The Alpine Planning Group
has held many special meetings and if one had been scheduled
between July 18 and July 27 the appointee would have been on
the Group Membership Roll as a member.

Planning Group Members are not "sworn in" nor are they
required to sign any oath of office. The sole authority for
the existence and functioning of the Alpine Planning Group [one
of many in San Diego County] is in Board of Supervisors Policy
I-1, a copy of which is attached. The County's planning groups
are part-time citizen advisory bodies elected by local regis-
tered voters and their members serve four year terms [Policy
I-1, pp. 2,3 & 4]. Planning Group Members have been made
subject to the Political Reform Act financial disclosure re-
quirements [Policy I-1, pp. 4 & 5].

Relevant references to the filling of Planning Group
vacancies have been made on pages 13 and 14 of Policy I-1. Please note
that Article III, Section III [page 13] states:

"Appointees to vacancies shall serve out the full
unexpired term of the vacant seat." [emphasis added].

A ruling on when this appointee "assumed office" would be
appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Attachments:  Board Minute Order #66 – July 18, 1989
              Board of Supervisors Policy I-1

HOWARD L. MCCALLA
2650 CALLE DE COMPADRES
ALPINE, CALIF. 92001

(619) 445-5738


September 26, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: John Larsen, Chairman
428 "J" Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

SUBJECT: Request for ruling on application of the term
"assuming office" [Gov. Code Section 87302(b)].

Dear Mr. Larsen,

     On July 18, 1989 the San Diego County Board of Supervisors
filled an existing vacancy on the Alpine Planning Group by
appointing an Alpine resident named Ulf Brynjestad. [See
attached Board minute order #66.] Financial disclosure state-
ments are required under Section 87302(b) "within 30 days of
assuming office...." The purpose of this letter is to request
an FPPC ruling on when this appointee is deemed to have
"assumed office". Was it the date of his appointment or was it
the date he attended his first meeting as a member.

     The appointee attended his first meeting on July 27, 1989,
the regular meeting for that month. The Alpine Planning Group
has held many special meetings and if one had been scheduled
between July 18 and July 27 the appointee would have been on
the Group Membership Roll as a member.

     Planning Group Members are not "sworn in" nor are they
required to sign any oath of office. The sole authority for
the existence and functioning of the Alpine Planning Group [one
of many in San Diego County] is in Board of Supervisors Policy
I-1, a copy of which is attached. The County's planning groups
are part-time citizen advisory bodies elected by local regis-
tered voters and their members serve four year terms [Policy
I-1, pp. 2,3, & 4]. Planning Group Members have been made
subject to the Political Reform Act financial disclosure re-
quirements [Policy I-1, pp. 4 & 5].

     Relevant references to the filling of Planning Group
vacancies are on pages 13 and 14 of Policy I-1. Please note
that Article II, Section III [page 13] states:

     "Appointees to vacancies shall serve out the full
unexpired term of the vacant seat." [emphasis added].

A ruling on when this appointee "assumed office" would be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature: Harold Burkhalter]


Attachments:     Board Minute Order #66 - July 18, 1989
                 Board of Supervisors Policy I-1

October 2, 1989


Howard L. McCalla
250 Calle De Compadres
Alpine, CA  92001

Re:  Letter No. 89-565

Dear Mr. McCalla:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on September 29, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Jonathan Rothman an attorney in the Legal
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[handwritten signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
